codeReview.summary: The fix removes the ignored packages/api/node_modules/drizzle-orm symlink and reroutes the team-invites predicate imports through a tracked @faworra-new/db seam, so it does address the original hidden-workaround failure. However, the feature still does not fully satisfy its own manifest/lock cleanup goal because bun.lock continues to record drizzle-orm as a direct packages/api dependency after the package.json removal.

issues.description: packages/api/package.json no longer declares drizzle-orm, but bun.lock still lists "drizzle-orm": "^0.45.1" inside the packages/api workspace dependency block. That leaves the repo's manifest/lock state inconsistent and still encodes the packages/api dependency drift that this fix feature was supposed to remove.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared validation contract does not currently check package manifest ↔ lockfile consistency, so dependency-seam fixes can leave stale workspace metadata behind unnoticed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The feature's verification steps in mission features.json cover symlink absence, typecheck, lint, and tests, and .factory/services.yaml lists only install/typecheck/lint/test/build commands. In the reviewed repo state, packages/api/package.json has no drizzle-orm entry, but bun.lock still records it under the packages/api workspace block at bun.lock:168.

summary: Reviewed the original failed feature and the fix rerun together. The fix successfully removes the ignored local drizzle-orm symlink and moves the API predicate import onto a tracked @faworra-new/db seam, but the review still fails because bun.lock was left stale and continues to encode drizzle-orm as a direct packages/api dependency.
